questions.question: Define data structure. Explain the importance of data structures in efficient data access and modification.
keyPoints: Definition of data structure as a way to organize, store, and manage data
Importance of data structures in efficient data access and modification
Example of how data structures affect program performance

questions.question: What is an Abstract Data Type (ADT)? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: ADT is a mathematical model defining data and operations independent of implementation
Specifies WHAT operations do, not HOW they are implemented
Example: List ADT with operations like insert, delete, search, traverse
Separates interface from implementation

questions.question: Compare and contrast arrays and linked lists in terms of their operations and time complexity.
keyPoints: Definition of arrays and linked lists
Comparison of operations: access, search, insertion, deletion
Time complexity analysis: O(1) for array access, O(n) for linked list search
Trade-offs: arrays for random access, linked lists for frequent insertions/deletions

questions.question: Explain the concept of Big O notation. List and compare common time complexities with examples.
keyPoints: Big O describes the upper bound (worst case) growth rate of an algorithm
O(1) - constant (array access), O(log n) - logarithmic (binary search)
O(n) - linear (linear search), O(n log n) - linearithmic (merge sort)
O(n^2) - quadratic (bubble sort), O(2^n) - exponential (recursive Fibonacci)
Ignores constants and lower-order terms

questions.question: Discuss the role of data structures in algorithm design. How does the choice of data structure affect the efficiency of an algorithm?
keyPoints: Relationship between data structures and algorithms
Choice of data structure determines applicable algorithms
Time complexity analysis: how data structure choice affects algorithm efficiency
Example: sorting algorithms and their dependence on data structures
